beats.activity: Wake up, make coffee, check messages and weather.
beats.reasoning: Low energy carryover from yesterday (35/100) means a gentle morning routine. Coffee and scrolling fits his pattern; the transit strike is already on his mind.

beats.reasoning: Short local walk avoids rideshare surge pricing and transit strike. Georgetown to Georgetown commute is walkable and fits his earlier pattern of enjoying the morning walk.

beats.reasoning: Yesterday's heat fatigue teaches him to head home early; Georgetown walk is short and avoids surge pricing. Partly cloudy means mild conditions but still worth escaping the office.

beats.reasoning: Low energy and yesterday's heat recovery pattern suggest an afternoon wind-down. Vinyl collecting and spreadsheet maintenance are solitary, restorative activities that fit his mood.

beats.activity: Browse records, chat with staff, enjoy the store energy.
beats.reasoning: Repeats his Day -2 pattern of finding solace and social connection at the record shop. Low energy but steady mood makes this a gentle, rewarding detour.

beats.activity: Walk back home via Capitol Hill and Georgetown.
beats.reasoning: Transit is back online at 4pm but walking is pleasant in mild evening weather. Avoids any remaining surge pricing and lets him decompress before dinner.

beats.activity: Cook a simple dinner, spin records, play online chess.; Continue playing chess online, scroll vinyl listings, wind down for bed.
beats.reasoning: Evening wind-down mirrors his Day -2 rhythm: cooking, vinyl, and chess. Low energy means no social plans or big outings; a quiet, restful evening at home.
7.5-hour sleep block allows recovery from yesterday's low energy. Ends before 06:30 per schema, spanning overnight as required.

diary:
Woke up to that perfect late-spring light filtering through the blinds—coffee tasted extra good this morning. The transit strike meant the streets were quieter on my walk to the office, almost peaceful. Work was solid; design review went smooth, and I managed to lock down some tricky user flow issues before lunch.

Grabbed a cold brew at Molly Moon's around midmorning, and the heat started getting to me. By late morning I just... left early. Needed the AC and some space to breathe. Put on an old Steely Dan record and got lost organizing my vinyl spreadsheet for like an hour—it's weirdly meditative.

The restlessness kicked in though, so I walked over to Sonic Boom in Capitol Hill. Just being around all those records and talking shop with the staff recharged something in me. Picked up nothing, but didn't need to. The evening dissolved into cooking, more records, some online chess. Feeling settled now, genuinely content.
thought_process: The transit strike actually worked in my favor today—I avoided the rideshare surge pricing by just walking everything, and Georgetown to the office is walkable anyway. I think I made a good call ducking out early when the heat got intense; I've learned that pushing through fatigue at work just ruins the rest of my day. The pull toward Sonic Boom was partly about records, sure, but more about needing to be around people and energy without the pressure of actual work interaction. Chess and vinyl organization scratch different itches—one's social, one's meditative. I ended up pacing my day better than usual, listening to what my body needed instead of grinding through.
world_event_prompt: Mild, partly cloudy Thursday, high of 68F. Citywide transit strike: all Metro buses and Link Light Rail suspended until 4pm. Rideshare surge pricing in effect.
